# Fully-resolved, hash-pinned closure of requirements.txt's dependency tree
# (including transitive deps), generated with Python 3.9 to match the actual
# Dockerfile base image (node:20-bullseye-slim's python3 is 3.9 — a lock
# generated under a different Python version can silently pick incompatible
# transitive versions, e.g. anyio releases that dropped 3.9 wheels; this bit
# us once, so regenerate only with 3.9). --require-hashes at install time
# means a compromised/re-published package on the index still gets rejected.
# Regenerate after changing requirements.txt:
#   apt-get install -y python3.9 python3.9-venv  # if not already present
#   python3.9 -m venv /tmp/lockenv && /tmp/lockenv/bin/pip install pip-tools
#   /tmp/lockenv/bin/pip-compile --generate-hashes --allow-unsafe \
#       --output-file=packages/core/requirements.lock.txt packages/core/requirements.txt
# (then re-add this header, since pip-compile overwrites the file verbatim)
#
